steps.title: Use the exponent rule
steps.content: A power of $\frac{1}{2}$ means square root. The exponent applies to the whole base $57y$: $$\left(57y\right)^{1/2}=\sqrt{57y}$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice C is $\sqrt{57y}$, which is exactly equivalent to $(57y)^{1/2}$. C is correct.

steps.title: Eliminate the mismatches
steps.content: Choice A incorrectly leaves $57$ outside the radical. Choice B incorrectly leaves $y$ outside the radical. Choice D squares the whole product before taking the square root, which would give $57y$, not $(57y)^{1/2}$.
